title: Republican Lawmaker Plans to Add Prediction Markets to Congressional Stock Ban Bill
summary: Rep. Bryan Steil plans to add prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi to a congressional stock ban bill. This move could impact how lawmakers participate in crypto-based betting platforms.
body:
Rep. Bryan Steil, a Republican lawmaker, announced plans to amend the House congressional stock ban bill to include prediction markets. These platforms, such as Polymarket and Kalshi, allow users to bet on future events using crypto.

The amendment aims to close a loophole that currently allows lawmakers to participate in these markets while banning them from trading individual stocks. Steil's proposal would extend the ban to cover prediction markets, ensuring that lawmakers cannot use these platforms to gain financial advantages based on insider information.

This change could significantly impact how lawmakers interact with crypto-based prediction markets. It would level the playing field by preventing them from profiting from their positions through these platforms. The amendment is part of a broader effort to increase transparency and reduce conflicts of interest in government.

The bill is expected to be voted on in the coming months. If passed, it would set a precedent for regulating prediction markets in the context of government ethics. Lawmakers and crypto enthusiasts should watch for updates on the bill's progress and potential amendments.
twitterThread: Rep. Bryan Steil wants to ban lawmakers from using prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi. This could change how politicians interact with crypto-based betting platforms.
The amendment to the congressional stock ban bill aims to close a loophole that currently allows insider trading through prediction markets. It's part of a push for greater government transparency.

If passed, this bill could impact how lawmakers use crypto. Watch for updates on the vote and potential amendments. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-04-republican-lawmaker-expands-stock-ban-bill-to-include-prediction-markets
standaloneTweet: Lawmakers might soon be banned from using prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi. This move aims to prevent insider trading and increase government transparency.
